text.title: Firefly Capability Map and Prompt Structure Rules
text.summary: Adobe Firefly capabilities across Creative Cloud applications, structured prompt requirements, and API access prerequisites.

list.item: Photoshop — Generative Fill (interactive), Background Removal, Object Selection AI; batch via Photoshop Actions or Firefly Services Batch API (enterprise)
Illustrator — Text-to-Vector (decorative quality, not production SVG icons); vector path complexity is high and paths are non-editable for UI use
Premiere Pro — AI editing assistance, scene detection
Adobe Express — template generation from text prompts

rule.statement: Every Firefly generation request must include a positive prompt, a negative prompt, a style preset (photo / art / graphic), and an aspect ratio. Requests without a negative prompt produce stock photo aesthetic defaults.
rule.rationale: Firefly is trained on Adobe Stock. Without exclusion constraints ("no watermark, no logo, no text, no realistic photo"), outputs default to generic stock photography that rarely matches brand guidelines.

rule.statement: Reference brand colors by name in prompts (e.g., "deep teal background"), not by hex value. Firefly interprets color names but ignores hex codes in prompts.
rule.rationale: Firefly's prompt parser has no hex color understanding. Hex values are silently ignored. Color names produce more consistent output even though post-processing color correction is often still needed.

rule.statement: Generate 3+ variants per prompt and have a human art director select before using any asset in production. Firefly output is non-deterministic — identical prompts produce different results.
rule.rationale: Non-determinism means no asset is guaranteed before generation. Single-variant generation creates bottlenecks when the single output is rejected at review.

rule.statement: Store generation prompts alongside assets in metadata (exiftool, C2PA content credentials) for reproducibility and content provenance documentation.
rule.rationale: Regulatory transparency requirements (C2PA) and internal reproducibility require that AI-generated assets carry generation provenance. Prompt metadata enables regeneration and audit trails.

rule.statement: Direct API automation of Firefly requires Adobe Firefly Services enterprise contract with beta access. Standard Creative Cloud accounts cannot trigger Firefly generation programmatically. Agent automation without this access is not possible.
rule.rationale: There is no public Firefly API for individuals as of 2026. Agents that claim to automate Firefly without enterprise access are using unofficial workarounds that violate Adobe ToS or are hallucinating the capability.

rule.statement: Track generative credit consumption before scheduling batch jobs. Credits expire and consumption per asset is not always predictable — running out during a campaign launch is a real operational risk.
rule.rationale: Generative credits are consumed per variation, not per prompt. A batch job requesting 10 variants per asset at 50 assets consumes 500 credits. Validate credit balance and rate before any production batch run.

text.reference: Generate structured Firefly Services batch API payload from content brief and brand guide
